notes: SHIP v1.14.250 — BOUNDARY FIXES: ngfw-4245 + as-2125 (MASTER_U_TABLE, data-only, height-only). Completes the sibling sweep .249 flagged. ⚠️ THE TWO RULES ARE NOT THE SAME SHAPE, AND RECON CHANGED THE PLAN — A PRIOR SELF-FLAG WAS PARTLY WRONG. .249's notes flagged BOTH rules as leaks needing a trailing boundary. Replaying the real masters BEFORE editing showed that would have been a BUG for as-2125. Real model strings: `ngfw-4245` is written EXACTLY that way (4 hosts, SPARKS s3:175); as-2125 is written `Supermicro AS-2125` (50 hosts, DFW02) but the RULED HARDWARE is the AS-2125HS-TNR — the master merely writes the short form today. (1) ngfw-4245 — was a BARE substring `/ngfw-4245/`, no guard on EITHER side, so `xngfw-4245` and `ngfw-4245-k9` silently inherited the 1U ruling. Now `/(^|[^a-z0-9-])ngfw-4245([^a-z0-9-]|$)/` — BOTH sides closed. Correct here because 4215/4225/4245 are DIFFERENT boxes; there is no family-prefix to preserve. (2) as-2125 — LEFT boundary ONLY. `\b` matches AFTER a hyphen as well as before one, so the old `/\bas-?2125/` let `foo-as-2125` inherit; that side is now closed with `/(^|[^a-z0-9-])as-?2125/`. ⭐ THE OPEN TAIL IS DELIBERATE AND WAS LEFT OPEN ON PURPOSE — DO NOT 'FINISH THE JOB' LATER. Closing it would stop matching the moment a master spells the fuller SKU (AS-2125HS-TNR, which is what the hardware actually IS), silently DROPPING 50 real hosts to unknown height — a regression from a correct known value. It is also right on the merits: Supermicro encodes U-height in the model number (AS-2125 => 2U), so the whole as-2125* family genuinely is 2U. A prefix match is the CORRECT semantics for this row; a bare substring was not. GUARD (both real masters replayed through the SHIPPED table vs the .249 baseline): HEIGHT DRIFT = 0 across ALL 6490 placed hosts on BOTH masters — real data bit-identical. The 4 ngfw-4245 hosts still resolve 1U; all 50 as-2125 hosts still resolve 2U. LEAKS CLOSED (were height-known under .249, now honestly unknown): `xngfw-4245` · `ngfw-4245-k9` · `foo-as-2125`. FAMILY BEHAVIOUR PRESERVED (still 2U, by design): `supermicro as-2125hs-tnr` · `as-2125gt-hnr` · `as2125` (hyphen-less spelling). CONTROL: `ngfw-4225` was unknown and remains unknown. GENERALISATION (now proven three times — .247, .249, .250): when a SKU contains hyphens, NEITHER a bare substring NOR `\b` is a model-exactness boundary; only an explicit negated class `[^a-z0-9-]` is. But WHICH SIDES to close is a per-rule judgement about the vendor's naming, NOT a mechanical rewrite — close the tail only where the SKU is exact, leave it open where the row is deliberately a family prefix. Gates: node --check x4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS, CSS brace balance 3856/3856 IDENTICAL (no CSS this ship), CRLF preserved (bare-LF 0), three-stamp lockstep .249 -> .250. ?legacy=1: unaffected (pure data table behind a pure classifier). ⛔ BATCH = 6 (.245 .246 .247 .248 .249 .250) — THE 6-SHIP CAP IS NOW REACHED. NO FURTHER SHIPS UNTIL JOHN'S DEVICE PASS (CALL 0). DEVICE-VERIFY (John), ONE pass covers all six: [.250] DFW02 -> the 50 `Supermicro AS-2125` rows still draw 2U to scale, no height hatch; SPARKS s3:175 -> the 4 ngfw-4245 rows still draw 1U (U38/U40), no hatch. Nothing else on either master may change height. [.249] SPARKS s3:175/s3:176 -> the 6 net-6x100g-02 rows still 1U, no hatch. [.248] BUILD subtab strip -> Crash-Cart pill GONE; tap Rack Map and Optics (the pills that flanked it) — neither dead-taps nor lands on SOPs; same under ?legacy=1 (pill gone there too, signed). [.247] s1:001 -> q3400-ra reads SWITCH + still gold hatch + MODEL HEIGHT UNKNOWN. [.246] s3:176 -> U31/U29/U28 read UNKNOWN gold, solid + to scale, no hatch; no tray prints 'undefined'. [.245] s3:176 U27 UFM reads SERVER. STILL OWED, JOHN'S RULING: the MEDIA-CONVERTER TYPE (net-6x100g-02 x6 + fs-media-converter-chassis x5 are both media converters per his 2026-07-14 call, but the app has NO media-converter type — needs his type + colour decision; heights already settled and must NOT be inferred from the type ruling) · DFW02's one empty-model row @c1:001:38 · the HEIGHT of q3400-ra (448 hosts still hatched).
